pages.full_text:
Saturday May 25th. Peter's birthday in Tibet.
	Campsite surrounded by hawthorne hedges being laid. Campsite owner enters hedging competitions. He gave us advice on laying out hedge - cut 2/3 through in spring when sap rising.
pages.notes: The page heading references 'Tibet' — this is likely a campsite name, not the country. No handwritten page number visible at top-left (may be obscured by gutter). The photo shows an aerial view of what appears to be a castle (likely Pembroke Castle given right-page context). Year inferred from right-page context (May 25th falling on a Saturday could be 1991, 1996, 2002 within the volume range; the right page mentions 'Trinity Sunday' for May 26th, consistent with 2002). Confidence in year is low.

pages.full_text:
We drove on to Pembroke Castle. It has the same layout as Sudley + Ludlow. The most amazing part of it is Wogan's Cavern, where supplies from boats were unloaded + carried up a spiral staircase into the castle. It is huge + was lived in for 1000 of years.
	We drove on to Solva, but rainsqualls were so heavy that we didn't get out of the van. We reached the campsite. Rhosson Gand ***** as good a crew as the Normandy campsite, but no lighthouse. Absolutely fabulous seascape. Heavy rain crashing waves.  __St. David's__

__Sunday May 26th__
	Overcast, but not raining. It was good to get out of the van. No sheep + we're the only people. So Tonic off the lead. We cycled off to the cathedral for an 11.15. Eucharist.
	The people are pretty short + the pews were too close together. Trinity Sunday. We
pages.notes: Written predominantly in pink/magenta ink. Handwritten page number '56' visible at top-right. 'Rhosson Gand' is likely 'Rhosson Ganol' (a campsite near St Davids, Pembrokeshire) — the asterisks may obscure part of the name or represent a rating. 'Sudley' may refer to Sudeley Castle (Gloucestershire). Text cut off at bottom ('Trinity Sunday. We'). 'Tonic' appears to be a pet name (dog). The phrase 'a good a crew' likely reads 'as good a crew' — possible misread, may be 'view'. Year assigned tentatively based on May 26th being a Sunday (Trinity Sunday 2002 = May 26) within volume date range.
